Transcrição Completa: 2 1 Mill a Month Talk with Zan Shaikh
[00:00] Thank you.
[00:01] Beautiful. I feel like this is giving me more dopamine than actually doing the Mel.
[00:05] Yeah. Yeah.
[00:06] I love it.
[00:07] Awesome. All right, everyone. So, um I was thinking about doing like the high-level lessons from from getting to this point. Hopefully, we do we'll do 2 million uh a month this month. We're aiming for like 2.5 uh contracted and 2 million collected.
[00:25] I'll uh walk you guys through the uh breakdown of the numbers so far in this month, but I wanted to just be a lot more mathematical because to be completely honest, what's got me to this point and what's going to keep us, you know, going forward is just math.
[00:40] You know, Jeremy, even two years ago in 2020 when I was doing 100k a month, you sat me down and we did the math about how to get to a million a month. And so two years later, after a lot of fuckups and and a lot of time, um we're here now.
[00:56] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Building The Machine"
NÚMEROS: 100K/month (2020), $1M/month target, 2 years timeline
VISUAL: Conceito de "máquina de fazer dinheiro", processo sistemático de crescimento de receita
[00:58] And so what what I really want to focus on today for all of you hopefully this is going to be a lot of numbers breaking it down really procedural and technical is building the machine right because I don't really think we appreciate how much value that Jeremy's actually brought to us because the like literally the ability to print money online is something that very few people have.
[01:20] And if you talk to anybody any boomer about what we do it breaks their reality because they had to do it the hard way right it's a lot harder for them to generate the kind of money that we generate today um because we have access to the internet and the advertising strategies.
[01:35] And then you, you know, pair that with a high ticket offer and you pair that with the proper math. You know, being able to generate really any number of of dollar amount is whatever.
[01:45] You know, I remember last mastermind you and I were chilling outside with uh Nick and Chares and I think Hust and you were like, "Yeah, I'm going to take this company to 10 million a month." And it was like whatever for you.
[01:58] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Scale = Spend More + Optimize"
NÚMEROS: $10M/month target
VISUAL: Mentalidade de escalabilidade, simplicidade matemática do crescimento
[02:00] You know, because you know, all it takes for you to get to that number is spend more money on ads and maybe a few optimizations on sales.
[02:08] So, first thing I'm going to give you a breakdown on um where we're at so far, just so you guys can kind of give a little bit of perspective.
[02:15] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Current Month Performance"
NÚMEROS: $130K signed today, $944K contracted (yesterday), $80.5K cash collected (yesterday)
VISUAL: Dashboard de performance mensal, breakdown de contratos vs cash coletado
[02:17] So, as of today, we had $130,000 contract get signed. So, this number is what I'm going to read you is reflecting yesterday. But, as of uh yesterday, we did 944K in contract value and 80.5K in cash collected and we did 130K in signed contract value today.
[02:38] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "The Business Machine Framework"
NÚMEROS: 4 componentes principais
VISUAL: Framework dividido em 4 pilares: Marketing | Sales | Delivery | Operations & Finance
[02:40] So um you know at the end of the day what what you really got to put into perspective is that the whole point of being in business if you want to keep creating more money or create create uh keep making more money is you have to build the machine.
[02:54] So what I uh kind of write the machine out is basically you know you've got your marketing your sales your delivery uh and your opfire operations and finance.
[03:05] And so the unfortunate thing is if you are lacking in any of these areas, what Ryan said earlier is what's going to happen to you and it's happened to me.
[03:13] I think even you Jeremy at some point has happened to you where you build up this business, you do all this stuff, something breaks and you're on the ground again. You're flat on your ass again. You're asking yourself what happened, you know.
[03:25] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Personality vs Business Failure Points"
NÚMEROS: Não especificado
VISUAL: Diagrama mostrando que força em Marketing/Sales leva a falha em Delivery/Ops, e vice-versa
[03:27] And usually if you're really good at this part of the if you're really good at this part of business, this part's going to be where you fail. And if you're really good at this part of business, generally this is where you fail.
[03:37] And and I think that just has to do with personality types and how you operate. To be completely honest, I hate detail-oriented [shit] I love being, you know, I'd rather sell all day and and, you know, market all day than worry about all the minutia.
[03:51] But guess what? If you don't have this part figured out, you're going to get a lot of calls, right? You're going to have a lot of people pissed at you and eventually you're not going to be in business for very long.
[04:02] So, what I ended up sitting down a few months ago, we were we were doing about 500 grand a month across a few offers.

NÚMEROS: $500K/month baseline
VISUAL: Auto-avaliação de valor no mercado, identificação de zona de gênio
[04:11] And I was sitting down, I was asking myself, okay, well, what is my true value prop in business? Like, if if somebody were to sit down with me and say, what am I valuable? Like, why why um, you know, would somebody pay me or what are my skills?
[04:25] For each and every single one of you, no matter where you're at in in the revenue level, is asking yourself, what's your value prop, right?
[04:32] For Jeremy, it's like he's the best in the world at at running advertising, you know, specifically for high ticket and personal brands.
[04:39] For, you know, Ryan, again, you were unbelievably good at generating clientele for your e-commerce brands.
[04:45] For me, again, same thing. Really, really damn [shit] good at marketing and sales.
[04:50] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Marketing & Sales Mastery = Never Below 100K"
NÚMEROS: $100K/month minimum após dominar marketing/sales
VISUAL: Linha de base de receita garantida após domínio de aquisição
[04:52] After we cracked 100 grand a month, I literally never did less than 100 grand a month on the offer. If anything, the issues are coming from, hey, we're bringing in too many clientele, and you need to work on delivering for these people more, otherwise you can't keep scaling.

NÚMEROS: Não especificado
VISUAL: Conceito de word-of-mouth invisível que acelera ou destrói crescimento
[05:09] And you're going to end up, you know, having uh really good video from Alex Herozi is he talks about the invisible word of mouth. That's the invisible hand that's either going to g uh uh gain growth in your business and grow your business forward or it's going to detract from your business, right?
[05:25] And I'm sure there's a bunch of names out there, you know, even some of the people we respect where you hear their name and there's some sort of negative connotation there, right? Because what did they they cared a lot about this and they cared very little about this.

[06:33] Um, it's something that I learned from Alex Becker, right? Is like that's all that matters in business is is recurring business, right?

NÚMEROS: $4M/month recurring revenue
VISUAL: Cenário hipotético: ads off + sales team gone = still $4M/month
[06:42] Because you you talk to Becker, he's doing I think four million a month right now. He turns his ads off tomorrow and his sales team dies in a plane crash, which God willing doesn't. He'll still make that four million every month, right?
[06:55] There's no Ryan, you and our sales guys uh get a little sick. You know, we're we're screwed, right? We're going to have a significant drop in revenue.

[07:16] Okay, guess what? If you're selling $1,000 a month to to run, you know, social media posting for some local business, unfortunately, I'm telling you, your business model isn't very scalable.

VISUAL: Foco em lane única: advertising mastery, sem stress de múltiplas operações

[07:37] And I think, okay, look at him. He's literally not handling. Maybe you do a little bit of sales consulting for your people too and operations consulting. But to be completely honest, he's doing his lane. He's sticking in his lane, making more money than all of us.
[07:50] And all of us are freaking stressed out all day because we're doing the bit. We're we're generating the marketing. We're doing the sales. We're delivering for the client. All this stuff.
[08:00] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Zone of Genius Principle"
NÚMEROS: Não especificado
VISUAL: Empresas maiores do mundo = cada pessoa faz apenas sua zona de gênio
[08:02] And so at the end of the day, if you look at the biggest companies in the world, the best companies in the world, not one person is doing everything. They're doing their zone of genius. They're playing their game and they're going off and making sure other people in their operation are getting what they're done.

NÚMEROS: Todas as ofertas
VISUAL: Decisão estratégica: contratar ou fazer parceria com times de delivery para cada oferta
[08:19] So, literally for every single one of my offers, I went out and found a delivery team. I either hired them in house or I partnered with them.

VISUAL: Análise de mercado: baixa competição, alto valor, puro lucro (sem inventário)

[08:43] And I started seeing like YouTube automation like what is this stuff, right? I started looking into it. I realized that hey, there's some significant advantages on the market because of the the value. You can charge a lot for it. You can make a lot.
[08:55] Most of the channels are making five to $15,000 a month in profit. They're not selling inventory. It's completely all profit. You're just making content and putting it online.

[09:24] And I I tell him look this is what I want to do. I'm going to give you the greatest offer in history, right? Cuz for me I'm all about leverage.
[09:32] Unfortunately, with more leverage, you you do take on more liability and you take on more um risk, unfortunately. But I never want to get fired, you know?
[09:42] I don't want to get [shit] fired. Like, if I work with somebody, I make him three million a month, and he just some, oh, take my ads, hire another media buyer, save, you know, 400 grand a month or whatever, I'm getting paid, and he just he just screws me over. I'm not interested in that personally.

[11:42] You don't pay me a retainer. I'm not a cost to you. You don't pay the ad dollars. I pay the ad dollars. You just make sure we liquidate the ad spend afterwards. But I get my chunk and I get paid the way I want to get paid.
[11:54] So that I'll do whatever I can to make you as much money. And literally at this point, I've grown the business to the point where there's literally no more clientele that we can take on per month until the operations grow.

NÚMEROS: Semanas de backup em clientes
VISUAL: Crescimento tão rápido que operações não conseguem acompanhar
[12:08] So now I'm I've already backed them up for, you know, for weeks, right? There's like there's no way that more business can be driven for this offer until the operations grow.
[12:18] So right now we're building out a a pretty big office in Australia to make sure we can continue to grow.
[12:23] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "The Math of Scaling"
NÚMEROS: 20x ROAS, $150K/month spend = $2M revenue, $1M spend = $20M revenue
VISUAL: Matemática simples de escalabilidade: aumentar spend = aumentar revenue proporcionalmente
[12:25] So that being said, what I did then was I just did the math, right? You do the math, you sit down, you have the machine, we say, "Okay, you know, we're going to right now our our rorowass is 20x. So for every dollar we put in, we're going to make $20.
[12:38] You know, we're spending maybe 150ish grand a month, so we make about two million. If I want to make $20 million in a month, okay, obviously these numbers will change at scale, but I just need to spend a million dollars roughly, give or take, and I'm going to make that number, right?
[12:54] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Marketing & Sales = Most Scalable"
NÚMEROS: Não especificado
VISUAL: Marketing/Sales escala mais rápido que qualquer outra parte do negócio
[12:56] So, the key thing about realizing once you've built the machine is that no person that could ever work with you will ever be able to keep up because actually marketing and sales once you have it down, it's the most scalable part of any business.
[13:09] It's the most scalable part of any business. Whether you like it or not, it's the thing that can grow the fastest, that can expand the fastest because unless it's a software company, ops don't ops isn't easy to scale.

[14:10] And then now that I have this machine, I don't really need to worry about the things that were causing me trouble on the way up because I've decided that's just not my that's not my lane anymore. You know what I'm saying?

[15:31] Dials for customer acquisition and dials for delivery. And as long as these two dials can get maxed out, technically you can just keep making as much money as you want because you just keep feeding the machine.
[15:42] Um, now that I'm I'm just going to pause here for a second because I'm going to talk about something even more important than customer acquisition.
[15:48] Is is everyone any questions? Maybe one or two questions. Hopefully I'm not confusing anyone.
[15:54] Okay, cool.
[15:56] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "The Machine Priority"
NÚMEROS: 3 pessoas vs 30 pessoas
VISUAL: Preferência: trabalhar com menos pessoas fazendo mais dinheiro em revshare
[15:58] So, first things first is having the machine. Every single one of us are learning marketing and sales. We're driving customers to businesses. Imagine if you could tweak your offer, however you do it, to make sure that you get a rev share or larger percent of rev share.
[16:13] Because to be honest, personally, I'd rather work with three people than 30 people. And I'd rather work with those three people and make a fuckload of money in their business than, you know, 10 people and I make them all a ton of money, but I'm only getting a piece.
[16:27] It's all personality. I just I've been screwed over too many times in life. I just I want to work with somebody.
[16:33] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Hostile Takeover Through Revenue Control"
NÚMEROS: Não especificado
VISUAL: Se todo revenue vem através de você, de quem é realmente o negócio?
[16:35] And the unique part about how I've structured everything is, you know, on whether it's fortunate or not, it's kind of like a hostile takeover, right?
[16:42] Because if you come over and you take a large, you know, you say, "Hey, I only want to get paid on what I bring in." But then you bring in all the revenue of that business, whose business is it really?
[16:52] You know what I'm saying? If the cash is coming in through my bank account, if I'm the one who's paying out the the the dollars, if the contracts are under my my system, if I own all the client data and I've signed an agreement saying that we're exclusive, you can't use any of my customers.
[17:08] Every single uh referral, reference, anything that comes from our customers comes to me. I get paid on everything. Whose business is it really?
[17:17] Um I'll leave that up, you know, to you to decide.

[17:23] Um, so I will never get fired. I don't care. I don't want to get fired. I don't want to deal with that. Like I don't want to have a client that I come on, bring him five million a month, and then he says, "Hey, you did a great job. Made me a ton of money, but you know, I'm I'm greedy."
[17:37] Which is fine. That's how humans act. So I'm just not going to let that happen to myself.

[17:44] And so now we talk about the next step, um, which is after you have the machine, which is the most important thing. You need to build the machine because without the machine, you don't have predictable revenue. You don't have the ability to turn the dial up.
[17:57] All Jeremy needs to do to make an extra million a month is go either find another client who he knows he can scale or talk to two of his clients and scale up the ad spend.
[18:06] So the next thing which is something that honestly I like you you shouted out Alex Hermoszi again. I think you should study everything Hermosi has. Watch his videos, read his books. He's obviously a very smart guy. He obviously makes more money than all of us. So read that book um and and watch his stuff.
[18:23] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "The Invisible Hand Concept"
NÚMEROS: Não especificado

[18:25] But one of the things that really really inspired me to become anal is absolute [shit] to become the best that I can not only in in generating business but making sure that my clientele are happy is he he talks about this concept of the invisible hand.
[18:40] Okay, there is an invisible hand in your business that is constantly pulling away from you or pushing you forward.
[18:47] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Entrepreneur Disappearance Pattern"
NÚMEROS: 30 pessoas desaparecidas desde 2016-2017
VISUAL: Padrão: empreendedor explode online, 2 anos depois desaparece completamente
[18:49] And you can actually very clearly see this in entrepreneurs where you see somebody who's like blowing up online and then two years later he's just gone.
[18:57] You know, I I've been in digital marketing since 2017 ever since I learned or maybe 2016 late whatever around then, right? Still here.
[19:05] I know I can name like 30 people off the top of my hands, but 30 people um that literally like I've met that are just not doing this anymore.
[19:14] And you ask yourself, okay, well, what what went wrong? It's this, right?
[19:18] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Word of Mouth = The Invisible Hand"
NÚMEROS: 90% do tempo você não está presente
VISUAL: Pessoas falando sobre você constantemente, sem você saber o que estão dizendo
[19:20] There's an invisible hand in your business, okay? And it's word of mouth. Whether you like it or not, especially in social media, people talk, people talk, people talk, people talk, people talk.
[19:30] And whether you like it or not, you're not there 90% of the time that they're talking about you. my name is probably being spoken this moment at this very moment and I don't know what the conversation is about. I hope it's good.
[19:42] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Client Experience > Customer Acquisition"
NÚMEROS: 5-10 anos máximo sem isso
VISUAL: Importância de experiência do cliente acima de fazer dinheiro rápido
[19:44] But that's why there's something even more important than customer acquisition. We don't think about because we're marketers and we don't really think, oh yeah, I just want to make money and cool great like I just made this guy a bunch of cash and and what now?
[19:57] Especially if you're the face like Ryan's the face of his business. He's trying to get out or you know Jeremy's the face for his personal brand. and I'm the face for my personal brand is if you do not pay immense attention to the word of mouth and the client experience and the feeling and the experience your clients have, you are [fucked]
[20:18] You're not going to be in business for more than 5 to 10 years. You will not. You will not. I don't care what happens.
[20:24] And if you do, you're probably going to have to change your name or work through a business partner where you're the guy in the background and nobody knows you.

[36:37] First is due diligence. It's your fault if he's not good. You know what I'm saying? If you if you're bringing business to somebody and and they're not going to know I God is literally like I said I need every single piece of proof you have.
[36:49] I can't ensue this relationship unless I know that I'm not going to screw myself because trust me, you don't want to do 20 million in sales and then have 10% even refund. It's going to destroy your business.
[37:01] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Leverage Reality"

[37:03] Um and then the second thing is leverage, man. Like people like to hear it, but you need it. You know, like how many when I when I was running my agency, even when I would do a good job, you know what I'm saying?
[37:14] Like if if this guy knew that he could make more money just by firing me and hire someone in-house. How many times probably Jeremy happened to you where you made this guy a little fuckload of money and then he's like, "Yeah, you know, I'll save like 300 grand a month uh you know firing you like I'll just take your ads and you know hope I do as good, right?"
[37:33] So like the car that's all piece of leverage is what you're saying.
[37:36] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Leverage Breakdown"
NÚMEROS: $2M/month → $0 se eu desaparecer
VISUAL: Eu pago ads, dinheiro entra na minha conta, meu CRM, minha sales team, meus dados
[37:38] Yeah. Like I spend the ad dollars. So I mean who dude he doesn't even have to worry about that. You know what I'm saying? And then all the money's coming into my bank account.
[37:46] So again like I'm the one who's controlling the revenue. um the the uh CL all the client data, all the customer data, all the CRM, all the sales people.
[37:54] Like if I disappear tomorrow, his revenue is going from 2 million or whatever it is to zero. So that's just like in reality, who has the leverage, right?
[38:03] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Brutal Honesty Principle"
NÚMEROS: Não especificado
VISUAL: Toda relação tem reacher e reached, leverage existe, honestidade brutal necessária
[38:05] And people don't like to hear this [shit] but like in every relationship almost always there's the reacher and the person who's reaching who has the leverage.
[38:13] And like people don't like to hear that stuff because it's brutally honest. But I'm somebody who like you can't affect life in real time unless you have brutal honesty.
[38:22] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Leverage vs Liability Trade-off"
NÚMEROS: Não especificado
VISUAL: Sem leverage = menos liability (Jeremy não vai a tribunal), com leverage = mais liability mas proteção
[38:24] So have the leverage however you can. And if you don't want the leverage, you get less liability. Like for example, if one of the companies Jeremy's scaling goes under, I mean, Jeremy's really he's not going to show up in court. He's not going to have to deal with [shit] I was just I was just doing my job, right?
[38:40] So he has less liability now.
[38:42] But he also has to deal with the fact that clients can walk out on him. You know, they wouldn't be smart. They'd be stupid to do it, but they can, right?
[38:51] So, it it's just some of those things. Any other questions?
[38:55] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Partners vs Clients Clarification"
NÚMEROS: 2 delivery partners
VISUAL: Rank One (delivery partner) + YTA (delivery partner) + outras in-house
[38:57] Well, I don't have clients. I have partners. Um I have Well, let's see. Um two partners, two two delivery partners, one for rank one and uh one for YTA. And then everything else is inhouse.
[39:11] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Partnership Split Breakdown"
NÚMEROS: Rank One: 60/40 (eu 60%), YTA: 50/50
VISUAL: Rank One = nossa empresa, não quis fazer delivery. YTA = já existia, entrei depois
[39:13] Yeah. With rank one, it's 60/40. I keep 60. the delivery team gets 40, but that's because we had that's literally our company, but I just didn't want to do the delivery anymore because I'd rather just focus on the marketing and sales um with with YTA, the company was already running. I came on board, so it's 50/50.
[39:31] Um and then uh there's, you know, some other offers that I have that I just own completely.
[39:36] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Zone of Genius Philosophy"
NÚMEROS: Não especificado
VISUAL: Capacidade humana limitada, cada um tem zona de gênio, jogar high leverage game
[39:38] But once they get to a certain uh big enough revenue, you just got to realize like humans all have different capacity. You have your zone of genius. You play the game that you played.
[39:47] Even Jeremy was giving us lessons about um you know uh the with the billionaire that's coming what is he he's like the master of high leverage actions. He's like asking himself how can I play at the highest leverage you know game that I can play.
[40:02] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Future Partnership Strategy"
NÚMEROS: 10%+ equity para sales guy, $100M/year target
VISUAL: Partners para cada negócio, vet intensamente, manter leverage, pagar bem
[40:04] Um so I'll probably end up having partners for for every business you know um but I will vet them like crazy. I'll make sure that I maintain the leverage but I'll also make sure they get paid incredibly well.
[40:17] You know like my sales guy technically he's a partner. Uh well now he runs my entire sales floor now and he's made like he's going to get over 10% of the company when it sells like but he's also going to take us to easily 100 million a year you know so um that's just some important things to keep in mind.
[40:36] Any other questions guys?
[40:38] Dope.
[40:40] Oh wait one more thing. Uh Husten had like one or two points that I think are really important.
📊 RESUMO EXECUTIVO
NÚMEROS PRINCIPAIS:
Receita Atual: $944K contracted + $80.5K cash collected (ontem) + $130K signed (hoje)
Meta Mensal: $2.5M contracted, $2M collected
Histórico: $100K/month em 2020 → $500K/month recente → $2M/month atual
ROAS: 20x ($1 gasto = $20 retorno)
Gasto em Ads: ~$150K/month atual
Linha de Crédito: $3M com MX
Float Máximo: $100K/month em ad spend
Canais YouTube: $5K-$15K/month profit por canal
Steady Stack: $2.5K/year algorithm, multi-6 figures/year pure profit
Client Retention: 20% compram outro canal ou referem em 1 mês
Response Time: 24 horas máximo
Onboarding: 10 minutos após pagamento
Sales Guy: 40s, multi-figure company experience, 10%+ equity
Estruturas de Split: Rank One 60/40, YTA 50/50
ESTRATÉGIAS E SISTEMAS:
Building The Machine Framework:
Marketing → Sales → Delivery → Operations & Finance
Dois dials: Customer Acquisition + Delivery
Ambos maximizados = receita infinita escalável
Partnership Leverage Structure:
0% retainer (não sou custo)
$0 ad spend do parceiro (eu pago, ganho pontos)
50% profit share em tudo que trago
Controle total: banco, CRM, sales team, client data
Exclusividade: todos referrals vêm para mim
"Hostile takeover" através de controle de revenue
The Invisible Hand (Alex Hormozi):
Word of mouth trabalha a favor ou contra
Client experience > customer acquisition
CAC aumenta inevitavelmente se experiência é ruim
30% talking shit = negócio destruído
Reputação = ceiling ou gap up
Client Experience Excellence:
Mahogany wooden plaques customizadas
Welcome packages automáticos via CRM
Confirmação de pagamento em 10 min (texto + email)
Onboarding completo em 10 minutos
Updates mensais via Loom video
Response time: 24 horas máximo
Linha direta pessoal para clientes
Client reps talentosos (investir mais que $2K-$3K/month)
Refund Philosophy:
Refund até break even se necessário
Problema é do negócio, não do cliente
Ter refund fund/chest dedicado
Protocolo: 1) Descobrir problema, 2) Consertar, 3) Devolver, 4) Ser classe
Refundados viram referrals futuros
Class act reputation = trabalhar novamente
Value Proposition Assessment:
Identificar zona de gênio pessoal
Focar apenas no que faz melhor
Delegar delivery completamente
Marketing/Sales = mais escalável que ops
Preferir 3 parceiros lucrativos que 30 clientes
Due Diligence Requirements:
Vet partners intensamente
Exigir toda prova de capacidade
$20M sales com 10% refund = destruição
Manter leverage sempre
Ethics & morality críticos no topo
Hiring Top Talent Strategy:
Vender visão vs dinheiro
Oferecer significância e acesso ao CEO
Equity na venda da empresa
Fazer pessoa sentir que importa
Aceitar menos dinheiro inicial por long-term
ESTRUTURA:
Ofertas Principais:
Rank One: Amazon coaching (60/40 split)
YT Automatic: YouTube automation (50/50 split)
Steady Stack: Options trading + algorithm ($2.5K/year recurring)
E-commerce Automation: Vários serviços
Modelo de Negócio:
Blue ocean markets (baixa competição)
High ticket offers ($70K wires mencionados)
Recurring revenue sempre que possível
Tech backend quando aplicável
Delivery terceirizado ou in-house
Operações:
Office na Austrália em construção
Sales floor completo
CRM automatizado para onboarding
Payment confirmation system
Client communication protocols
Refund fund dedicado
DADOS FINANCEIROS:
Receitas:
$944K contracted (ontem)
$80.5K cash collected (ontem)
$130K signed (hoje)
Target: $2.5M contracted, $2M collected/mês
Multi-6 figures/year em Steady Stack (puro lucro)
Custos:
~$150K/month ad spend atual
$2K-$3K/month+ para client reps (investir mais)
Float máximo: $100K/month
Margens:
20x ROAS atual
$5K-$15K/month profit por canal YouTube
100% profit em algorithm sales (sem inventário)
Escalabilidade:
$1M ad spend = ~$20M revenue (teoria)
$10M/month target com partner atual
$100M/year target com sales guy
Splits:
Rank One: 60% (Zan) / 40% (delivery)
YTA: 50% (Zan) / 50% (partner)
Sales guy: 10%+ equity na venda
DURAÇÃO: ~41 minutos FORMATO: Mastermind talk / Workshop sobre construção de negócios escaláveis de 7-8 figuras
